sound_type: group howl -- ascending yip-howl chorus. 300-3,000 Hz. 3-7 animals sound like 20. Frequency-hopping. 3-5+ mile carry. E=6
ecosystem: Varied -- forest, grassland, urban, suburban, alpine, desert. Most adaptable large carnivore. Year-round resident.
season: Year-round. Howling increases breeding (Jan-Mar) and fall dispersal.
family: Canidae (RETURNS -- last at degree 71, Red Fox. 137-degree gap)

coordinates.description: Coyote in PNW -- urban, suburban, forest, alpine. Everywhere. Group howl audible from any suitable habitat.

taxonomy_note.description: CANIDAE RETURNS. Last at degree 71 (Red Fox). 137-degree gap -- longest family gap in engine.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Canidae: Red Fox Bark (71, E=3), Coyote Group Howl (208, E=6).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Canis from Latin 'dog'. latrans from Latin 'barking' -- the barking dog.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Canis -- true dogs. Coyote is one of several Canis species (with wolves, jackals).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Canidae returns. Most vocal North American canid.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Canidae: second entry. 137-degree gap. Energy arc: E=3 (fox) to E=6 (coyote).
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Most adaptable North American carnivore. Every habitat type. Persecution resilient.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 300-3,000 Hz. Wide range. Multiple animals, different pitches.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Group howl: 300-3,000 Hz. Solo howl: 400-1,500 Hz. Bark: 500-2,000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual howl: 3-10 sec. Group chorus: 30 sec to minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: Yip prelude (staccato) escalating to ascending howl (frequency-modulated). Overlapping pack voices.
song_metrics.note_types: Group howl (ascending chorus), solo howl (contact), bark/yip (alarm), whine (submission).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Yips -> ascending howls -> wavering sustain -> fade. Pack members stagger entry. Complex overlap.
song_metrics.timbre: Rich, wavering, frequency-hopping. Multiple harmonics. Each animal sounds like 2-3. Pack sounds like 15-20+.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Maximum canid energy. Valley-filling. 3-5+ mile carry. Most iconic mammalian sound in N.A.
song_metrics.tempo: Yips: rapid (4-8 per second). Howls: sustained (3-10 sec). Group tempo: overlapping, no unified beat.
song_metrics.context: Territorial assertion. Group reunion. Breeding season. Dusk/dawn/night.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Urban coyotes may howl less frequently (avoiding detection). Rural: full vocal repertoire. Pitch and rhythm vary by region.
song_metrics.key_property: GROUP HOWL. Ascending yip-howl. E=6. CANIDAE RETURNS (137-degree gap). Frequency-hopping. 3 sounds like 20. 3-5+ mile carry. Most iconic. v1.49.349.

pelage.overall: Gray-brown to yellowish-gray. Lighter belly. Bushy tail with black tip. 8-20 kg. Slender, dog-like.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Slender canid with pointed ears, narrow snout, bushy tail with black tip. Smaller than wolf. Larger than fox.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Winter: thicker, grayer coat. Summer: thinner, more yellowish. Tail bushy year-round.

physical_description.overall: 8-20 kg (18-44 lbs). 75-100 cm body + 30-40 cm tail. Slender. Pointed ears. Narrow snout. Bushy black-tipped tail.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Slender dog-like canid. Smaller than wolf, larger than fox. Pointed ears erect. Narrow face. Running gait: tail held low (wolf holds tail high).
physical_description.field_id: Slender canid on trail, field, or road. Pointed ears, narrow face, bushy tail. At dawn/dusk/night, listen for ascending yip-howl chorus. In urban areas, may appear on streets at dusk.

nodes.title: Canidae Returns -- 137-Degree Gap
nodes.description: Last Canidae at degree 71 (Red Fox, E=3). Longest family gap. Coyote at E=6. Full family vocal range: solitary bark to valley-filling chorus.

nodes.title: Acoustic Deception -- Frequency-Hopping
nodes.description: Each animal frequency-hops. One sounds like 2-3. Pack of 3-7 sounds like 20. Force multiplier for territorial defense. Social intelligence.

nodes.title: Ultimate Generalist -- Every Habitat
nodes.description: Forest to urban. Alaska to Mexico. Most adaptable large carnivore. Every US state except Hawaii. Behavioral shift in cities.

nodes.title: Persecution Resilience -- Population Paradox
nodes.description: Century of killing increased populations. Larger litters compensate. Range expanded during persecution. Remarkable ecology.

cross_references.relationship: CANIDAE REUNION! 137-degree gap. Solitary bark (E=3) to group chorus (E=6). Longest family gap in engine.
Both E=6. Impact force (ram) vs. vocal force (coyote). Different paths to maximum energy.
Eerie moans (E=3) to iconic howls (E=6). Night sounds. Porcupine unsettles, Coyote commands.
VOICE FILLS SPACE! Soul baritone fills venue. Coyote chorus fills valley. Both E=6. Total E=12. Spatial illusion.
